claim: Runtime and CLI symmetry is required for trustworthy hook execution.
mechanism: If the runtime invokes hooks differently from the canonical CLI path, a chip can appear valid in one surface and broken in another. Aligning the runtime with the file-based CLI contract removes that split-brain behavior, lowers integration ambiguity, and makes local verification far more predictive of real operation.
boundary: This packet applies to CLI-style hook commands. Non-CLI subprocess patterns may still require fallback handling.
